A Life Beautifully Lived
Biography
Mr. Edward Kwame Akoto Danso  ·  1948 – 2025
In His Early Years

The late Mr. Edward Kwame Akoto Danso was born on 8th October 1948 at Kwahu Mpraeso in the Kwahu South Municipality of the Eastern Region of Ghana, to Madam Juliana Abena Dansoa and Mr. Yaw Badu Danso. From childhood, he exhibited remarkable talents, profound humility, quiet obedience, and a deep respect for both elders and peers. He was known for his disciplined nature and his abiding love for education, always approaching schooling with enthusiasm and unwavering commitment.

He began his formal education at Mpraeso Methodist School, where he completed his elementary education in 1965. His school years were filled with warmth and activity. Among his greatest passions was football — he delighted in playing with his schoolmates on the school park after lessons. This love for the game endured throughout his life, and he remained a devoted supporter of Accra Hearts of Oak Sporting Club.

After completing his education, he moved to Adieso to live with his uncle, the late Mr. Bediako, who became a formative figure in his life. Under his uncle's careful guidance, he gained invaluable experience in business discipline, personal responsibility, and perseverance — a solid foundation upon which his future would be built.

In Traditional Attire

Through hard work, determination, and an enduring spirit, Mr. Edward Kwame Akoto relocated to Nsawam, where he established his own hardware business. He engaged in both the distribution and retail of hardware products, becoming widely respected for his honesty, diligence, and exemplary customer relations. As the enterprise flourished, he brought in his younger brother to help manage the growing business.

Beyond commerce, he was widely admired for his generosity and compassionate spirit. He consistently extended a helping hand to farmers in his community, assisting them in seeking financial support to improve their livelihoods. He also remained steadfastly committed to the wellbeing of his parents, siblings, and relatives whenever the need arose.

In 1980, he married Mrs. Victoria Owusu, and together they built a loving and united family. Their union was blessed with children whom he loved dearly and worked tirelessly to nurture, educate, and provide for. Though political instability during Ghana's coup d'état era brought painful setbacks, he remained resilient and hopeful. He eventually settled the family at Akyem Osenase, where he devoted himself to farming — continuing to lead a hardworking, responsible life in service of his household.

On 29th November 2025, he was taken ill and admitted to St. Dominic Hospital. He initially responded positively to treatment, giving hope to family and loved ones. Sadly, on 2nd December 2025, his condition worsened, and he peacefully passed on to glory.

The passing of Mr. Edward Kwame Akoto Danso is a profound loss to his family, his community, and all who knew him. He will forever be remembered for his humility, resilience, generosity, and tireless dedication to the wellbeing of others.

REST WELL, KWAME AKOTO · YOUR MEMORY LIVES ON

Words from the Heart
Tributes
✦
Tribute by
The Family
"The sun rises and the sun goes down, and hastens to the place where it rises."— Ecclesiastes 1:5

It is with deep sorrow, yet with gratitude to God for a life well lived, that we pay tribute to our beloved father, brother, uncle, and friend — Opanyin Kwame Akoto, a proud son of Kwahu, hailing from Mpraeso.

Kwame Akoto, as we affectionately called him, was a devoted father who cherished family unity, respect, and the dignity of honest labor. To us, he was more than a father and a brother; he was a mentor, a protector, and an enduring source of wisdom. His quiet strength, kindness, and sacrifices shaped the lives of his children and all who had the privilege of knowing him.

He taught us the value of perseverance and the importance of caring for one another. Though our hearts are heavy with grief, we are comforted by the beautiful memories he leaves behind. His life was truly a blessing to us, and his legacy will continue to live on through his children and the entire family.

Daddy, you were indeed a precious gift from God to our family. Your love, guidance, and sacrifices will never be forgotten. Though you are gone from our sight, you will forever remain in our hearts.

Kwame Akoto, demirefa due  ·  Wofa Akoto, Rest in Perfect Peace.
♡
Tribute by
His Beloved Wife, Mad. Victoria Owusu
"Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of His faithful servant."— Psalm 116:15

Edward Kwame Akoto Danso, whom I fondly called "Daa," was a God-fearing, loving, brave, and caring father to many. Above all, he was the love of my life. I began calling him "Daa" because he became not only my husband, but also my protector, my companion, and the steadfast father of our children. We met, became friends, and that friendship soon blossomed into marriage in 1980. He treated me with great love and care throughout every season of our years together.

My husband was an admirable and wonderful man — soft-spoken, a natural peacemaker, and a man of deep understanding. His wisdom kept love, faith, and hope alive throughout our marriage. He was my comforter in times of distress, and his counsel was always filled with patience and genuine care.

"Daa" was a man of dignity and self-respect. One thing I deeply admired was the intentional way he raised our children to become responsible and respectful adults. He also cared deeply about the growth of my business, often asking, "Are you keeping proper records?" and patiently helping me update the customer record books he had created for me — reminding me always that proper bookkeeping reveals whether one is truly making progress.

The 2nd of December 2025 became a heartbreaking day — you left without saying goodbye. "Daa," you touched every aspect of my life. Though death has taken you from my sight, it can never take you from my heart and soul. I hold firmly to the promise of eternal life — that one day we shall meet again, where there will be no sorrow, no pain, but only everlasting joy in the presence of the Lord.

Nanti yie, Mewura. Nanti yie, Medofo pa.
M'adepa, gye wo ahome wo Awurade kokomu, kosi se yebehyia mu bio wo soro.
Words from the Heart
Tributes (continued)
✦
Tribute by
His Children
"Everything that happens in this world happens at the time God chooses."— Ecclesiastes 3:1

Words cannot fully express how precious you were to us, Daddy. You were our peace, our adviser, our mentor, our best friend — and in our hearts, we saw a leader, an image of God. You were a man who would go to the very limit, even selling your last possession, to fulfill the promises you made to us. Above all, you were our hero.

Daa, you never left us alone, even as we grew older. Our welfare mattered so deeply to you. Whenever we said we were coming home, you never stopped calling to find out how far along we were. When we were unwell, you felt our pain and did everything within your power to see us restored to health.

That dark Tuesday was the last day we saw you and heard your voice. Our backbone was broken. You left without telling us where you were going — as you always did whenever you ventured out. We had promised each other that we would enjoy the better days the Lord had prepared for us, together.

Daddy, your passing has shaken us deeply, but we are grateful to God for giving us such a wonderful and noble father — a man known for peace, unity, and love. We will never forget your encouragement and motivation. If we had the chance to choose, we would choose you a million times over.

Our hero, we love you. God loves you more.
Daa, demirefa due. Onyankopon mfa wo nsie yie, kosi se yebehyia mu bio.
✦
Tribute by
His In-Laws
"For the upright man is taken away from evil; he enters into peace — those who walk in integrity rest on their couches."— Isaiah 57:2

Mr. Edward Kwame Akoto Danso was an exceptional man whose life touched many in profound and meaningful ways. We called him "Daddy" because of the fatherly role he played in all our lives. He was a man of peace, wisdom, humility, and deep contentment. He welcomed everyone with warmth and sincerity, making all who came into contact with him feel accepted and genuinely valued.

One remarkable quality that set him apart was his genuine concern for family unity. Whenever he spoke with his daughters, he would consistently inquire about the wellbeing of his in-laws — revealing the weight he placed on togetherness, mutual respect, and family bonds. He treated everyone with fairness and dignity, never allowing division to take root around him.

As a hardworking and selfless man, Daddy believed strongly in honest labor and contentment. He lived a simple but honorable life and never measured relationships by material gain. Even in difficult moments, he remained grateful and steadfast, teaching all around him the true value of humility and simplicity.

To us, he was more than an in-law. He was a good man — a mentor, a peacemaker, and a treasury of wisdom and encouragement. His kindness, selflessness, and calm nature will forever remain engraved in our hearts.

Daddy, your labor, wisdom, love, and peaceful spirit will never be forgotten.
Rest peacefully in the bosom of the Almighty, until we meet again.
Words from the Heart
Tributes (continued)
✦
Tribute by
His Grandchildren

We did not fully realize how much of our happiness was tied to you until you were gone. Your love was quiet but strong, and it wrapped around us in ways we are only now beginning to understand.

You were always there — with your gentle smile, your wise words, and your warm presence that made everything feel safe and well. Being your grandchildren is a blessing we will forever be grateful for. The memories we shared with you may seem small to others, but to us, they mean everything.

Our Grandpa always made sure we never went hungry. Whenever we returned from school, he would have food set aside for us — and if it had run out, he would quietly give us money to buy biscuits. He guided us with love, patiently teaching us to be good children and to honor our elders.

Though you are no longer with us, your love lives on in our hearts. We are grateful for every moment we shared with you.

Sleep peacefully, Grandpa. You will never be forgotten.
✝
Tribute by
St. Theresa Catholic Church, Akyem Osenase

Today, we gather with heavy hearts but deep gratitude to honour a devoted parishioner, a committed servant of God, and a true pillar of our church community — the late Edward Akoto Danso.

Edward Akoto Danso was not merely a member of St. Theresa Catholic Church; he was a dedicated steward in the vineyard of the Lord. His life was marked by humility, a quiet sense of responsibility, and an unwavering commitment to the growth and development of the Church. During his tenure as Financial Secretary, he served with diligence, transparency, and integrity.

One of the most enduring achievements of his service was the successful acquisition of twenty pews for the Church — a lasting legacy that created comfort and dignity for worshippers, and whose benefit the congregation continues to enjoy to this day. This was more than a physical contribution; it was an act of foresight and love for God's house.

Edward was a man of peace, respect, and quiet strength. He related well with all — young and old alike — and carried himself with a spirit of genuine service that inspired many. He gave not only his time and skills, but his heart to the community of faith.

Though we mourn his passing, we also celebrate a life well lived — a life of service, faith, and dedication to God and His people. As it is written, "Well done, good and faithful servant… enter into the joy of your Lord." (Matthew 25:23)

Mr. Edward Akoto Danso, your work here is done, but your legacy remains.
May the Lord grant you eternal rest, and may perpetual light shine upon you.
Rest in Perfect Peace.
Songs of Faith & Hope
Hymns
Catholic Hymn 212 · Processional
Yes, Heaven Is the Prize
Yes, heaven is the prize,
My soul shall strive to gain;
One glimpse of Paradise
Repays a life of pain.
'Tis Heaven; yes, heaven;
Yes, heaven is the prize;
'Tis Heaven; 'tis heaven;
Yes, heaven is the prize.
Yes, heaven is the prize!
When sorrows press around,
Look up beyond the skies,
Where hope and strength are found.
Yes, heaven is the prize!
The strife will soon be past,
Faint not, but raise your eyes,
And struggle to the last.
Catholic Hymn 349 · Communion
Abide with Me
Abide with me; fast falls the eventide;
The darkness deepens; Lord, with me abide.
When other helpers fail and comforts flee,
Help of the helpless, O abide with me.
I fear no foe, with you at hand to bless;
Ills have no weight, and tears no bitterness.
Where is death's sting? Where, grave, your victory?
I triumph still, if you abide with me.
Hold up your cross before my closing eyes;
Shine through the gloom and point me to the skies.
Heaven's morning breaks, and earth's vain shadows flee;
In life, in death, O Lord, abide with me.
Catholic Hymn 305 · Recessional
Hark, Hark, My Soul
Hark, hark, my soul! Angelic songs are swelling,
O'er earth's green fields, and ocean's wave-beat shore;
How sweet the truth those blessed strains are telling,
Of that new life when sin shall be no more!
Angels of Jesus, angels of light,
Singing to welcome the pilgrims of the night!
Rest comes at length, though life be long and dreary;
The day must dawn, and darksome night be past;
All journeys end in welcome to the weary,
And heaven, the heart's true home, will come at last.
Catholic Hymn 351 · Communion
Lead, Kindly Light
Lead, kindly Light,
Amid the encircling gloom,
Yes, lead me on!
The night is dark,
And I am far from home;
Yes, lead me on!
Keep firm my feet;
I do not ask to see
The distant scene;
One step enough for me.
So long your power has blessed me, sure it still
Will lead me on.
O'er moor and fen, o'er crag and torrent,
Till the night is gone,
And with the morn, those angel faces smile,
Which I have loved, long since, and lost awhile!
Catholic Hymn 350 · Communion
Guide Me, O Thou Great Redeemer
Guide me, O you great Redeemer,
Pilgrim through this barren land;
I am weak, but you are mighty;
Hold me with your powerful hand.
Bread of heaven,
Feed me now and evermore.
When I tread the verge of Jordan,
Bid my anxious fears subside;
Death of death, and hell's destruction,
Land me safe on Canaan's side.
Songs of praises,
I will ever give to you.
Catholic Hymn 34 · Responsorial Psalm
My Soul Is Longing for Your Peace
Antiphon: My soul is longing for your peace,
Near to you, my God.
Lord, you know that my heart is not proud,
And my eyes are not lifted from the earth.
In your peace I have maintained my soul,
I have kept my heart in your quiet peace.
As a child rests on his mother's knee,
So I place my soul in your loving care.
Israel, put all your hope in God,
Place your trust in him, now and evermore.
With Gratitude
Acknowledgement

The Royal Asona Gyase Family of Abusua Panyin Bamfo Wiafe, together with the entire family of the late Mr. Edward Kwame Akoto Danso, wishes to express its sincere and heartfelt gratitude to all who stood with them during this season of grief.

To every family member, friend, colleague, church associate, well-wisher, and sympathizer who traveled near and far, sent condolences, made donations, or offered prayers — your love, solidarity, and support have been an immeasurable source of comfort and strength.

We are especially grateful to the leadership and congregation of St. Theresa Catholic Church, Akyem Osenase, and all officiating clergy, for the grace and spiritual care extended to us.

"May the God of all comfort be with you, as you have been with us."

2 Corinthians 1:3–4

God richly bless you all.

The Family of
Mr. Edward Kwame Akoto Danso
Those Who Grieve

Obaapanyin Yaa Akyeoa, U.K. Obaapanyin Annorbea, Accra.

V. Rev. Fr. Augustine Frempong (Parish Priest), Akwatia. Abusua Panyin Kofi Danso. Obaapanyin Acheampomaa, Nana Gyaase (Oduro Safo). Abakoma Asirifi Baah. Obaapanyin Yaa Brago, Akosua Ankomaah. Obaapanyin Afia Kwakyewaa. All of Aduana Royal Family, Akyem Osenase.

Ahenemma:
Akosua Agyarawaa, Akua Effah, Ama Serwaa, Kwahu Kissi, Akosua Asantewaa — all of Mpraeso-Kwahu.

Eternal Rest

Eternal rest grant unto him, O Lord,
and let perpetual light shine upon him.

May he rest in peace. Amen.

May his soul, and the souls of all the faithful departed,
through the mercy of God, rest in peace. Amen.

All Friends and Sympathizers Are
Cordially Invited
